choice: Store the E*TRADE connection/code per household — it is not universal across households, so scope the credentials to the household rather than as instance-wide app config.
rationale: Human-chosen during grooming (overrode recommendation: Use a single app-level E*TRADE consumer key/secret (registered once by the instance owner, stored as app config outside any household partition), and a standard 3-legged OAuth 1.0a per-user handshake: app gets a request token → user is sent to E*TRADE → user pastes back the verifier code → app exchanges for the daily access token (encrypted per e540d090). Because tokens die at midnight ET with no refresh (db982dac), 'Reconnect E*TRADE' (b45b7691) re-runs exactly this verifier flow; there is no silent renewal. Treat the verifier paste-back as expected friction, surfaced as a clear daily reconnect prompt, not an error.)
